# Private School Operator — Nigeria-First Research Brief

**Niche ID:** P3-private-school-private-school-site
**Vertical slug:** private-school
**VN-ID:** VN-EDU-PRV-001
**Niche family:** NF-EDU-PRV anchor (nursery-school variant depends on this)
**Family role:** anchor
**Research date:** 2026-04-26

## 1. Nigerian Business Reality
Nigeria has over 60,000 private schools (nursery, primary, secondary) concentrated in urban areas. Private schools account for over 60% of school enrolment in Lagos, Abuja, and Port Harcourt. They are regulated by State Ministries of Education, SUBEB (Universal Basic Education Board) for primary, and WAEC/NECO registration for secondary. Annual school fees range from ₦200,000 (basic) to ₦3M+ (premium boarding schools). Key selling points are WAEC result quality, discipline, teachers' credentials, and extracurricular activities. Parents are the primary decision-makers; students search through Google and word-of-mouth.

## 2. Target Audience Profile
- **Mrs Blessing Osei, 42, Parent, GRA Ikeja Lagos** — Seeking a secondary school for her JSS1 child. Prioritises WAEC results, security, and subject availability. Will WhatsApp the school for a tour.
- **Mr Salihu Garba, 48, Parent, Kaduna** — Wants an affordable primary school with Islamic Studies option. Needs fee schedule and admission form.
- **Olamide Adeyemi, 35, Head Teacher** — Looking for an LMS/admin platform. This template is the school's public website to attract new students.

## 3. Recommended Website Structure
- **Home:** Hero (graduating students, proud parents), WAEC results badge, admission CTA, WhatsApp
- **About:** School history, proprietor profile, mission/vision, Ministry of Education licence
- **Academics:** Curriculum (NERDC/CBE), WAEC/NECO, extracurricular, school calendar
- **Admissions:** Fee schedule (term fees in NGN), requirements, application form link
- **Gallery:** Classrooms, labs, sporting events, graduation ceremonies
- **Contact:** WhatsApp, phone, address, NDPR notice

## 4. Content Tone & Voice
Aspirational, trustworthy, parent-facing. "Excellence. Discipline. Character." register. Reference WAEC/NECO by name. Primary CTA: "Apply for Admission". Secondary CTA: "Book a School Visit". WhatsApp CTA: "Chat with Admissions Team".

## 5. Trust Signal Inventory
- State Ministry of Education operating licence number
- SUBEB registration certificate (primary)
- WAEC/NECO school registration code (secondary)
- WAEC results pass rate display (year by year)
- CAC company/NGO registration
- Years of operation
- Staff qualification badges (NCE, B.Ed, PGDE)
- Payment: school fees via bank transfer, Paystack, USSD

## 6. Image Direction (5-7 Specific Concepts)
1. Students in school uniform on morning assembly — diverse Nigerian student body, Nigerian flag in background
2. WAEC results celebration — students with certificates, excited parents
3. Classroom learning — attentive students, teacher at chalkboard, natural light
4. School sports day — athletics track, colourful team jerseys
5. Science laboratory — students conducting experiments, safety goggles
6. School gate with signboard — neat compound, uniformed security
7. PTA meeting — parents and teachers in school hall, collaborative atmosphere

## 7. Regulatory Compliance Checklist
- State Ministry of Education operating licence displayed
- SUBEB registration for primary schools
- WAEC registration code for secondary schools
- UBEC compliance (free UBE access notice)
- NDPR consent for student application data collection
- Child protection/safeguarding policy reference

## 8. Nigeria-First Compliance Checklist
- [x] WhatsApp CTA on home page and contact page
- [x] All fees in NGN (₦) per term
- [x] Nigerian context (GRA Ikeja, Kaduna, WAEC, NECO)
- [x] Nigerian names (Mrs Blessing, Mr Salihu) in testimonials
- [x] Ministry of Education licence trust signal
- [x] Payment: bank transfer, Paystack, USSD for school fees
- [x] Mobile-first 375px layout
- [x] esc() on all user-supplied strings
- [x] NDPR notice in admission application forms
